posts.author: CS_Sainath
posts.text: Zerodha Coin: Is Minimalism Turning into Neglect? — Appreciate the detailed and transparent roadmap; it’s great to see Zerodha prioritizing a unified, goal-based experience for long-term investors, I look forward to seeing these updates roll out.

posts.author: Neelesh
posts.text: Zerodha Coin: Is Minimalism Turning into Neglect? — Thanks for the detailed feedback. Most of what you’ve raised is valid, and we’re already working on it. On features - We’re building goal-based investing, better analytics, and SIP improvements. Some of it is already in testing internally. On the fragmented experience - We’re bringing the full mutual fund flow into Kite. Buy, sell, SIP, track, all within Kite. The Coin itself is getting a complete revamp, with MF, FD, and NPS all sitting in one portfolio view. The app juggle is going away. On FD and NPS - We’re rebuilding both to be fully nat…

posts.author: CS_Sainath
posts.text: Zerodha Coin: Is Minimalism Turning into Neglect? — As a long-term user of the Zerodha ecosystem, I’ve always appreciated the “no-nonsense” philosophy. However, there is a fine line between being minimalistic and being outdated. For those of us who primarily invest in Mutual Funds, Zerodha Coin is starting to feel like a platform stuck in the past. 1. The Feature Vacuum: Groww, Kuvera, and INDmoney are Miles Ahead While Zerodha was a pioneer in direct mutual funds, it has barely evolved. Competitors like Groww , Kuvera , and Scripbox offer a suite of tools that make Coin look like a basic spre…
